extracted.brandBackground: Sky Zone operates indoor trampoline parks featuring trampoline attractions and other active entertainment attractions.

leadership.name: David Hoffmann
leadership.background: Served as CEO of Mammoth Holdings Inc. from October 2021 to May 2025.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides executive leadership and strategic direction for the parent company.

leadership.name: Mike Revak
leadership.role: Chief Operating Officer, CTH and SFG
leadership.background: Previously served as President of SFG and COO of Rockin' Jump Franchise, LLC.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees daily operations and franchise development across the brands.

leadership.name: Michael Healy
leadership.role: Chief Financial Officer, CTH and SFG
leadership.background: Served as Chief Financial Officer for Bloomin' Brands from May 2009 to October 2025.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs financial strategy, accounting, and fiscal management.

leadership.name: Stephanie Meltzer-Paul
leadership.role: Chief Commercial Officer, CTH and SFG
leadership.background: Previously served as Executive Vice President of Global Loyalty Services for Mastercard.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads commercial strategy, marketing, and guest experience initiatives.

leadership.name: Sherin Sakr
leadership.role: President of International and Chief Legal Officer, CTH and SFG

leadership.whyItMatters: Manages international expansion and all legal and regulatory affairs.

leadership.name: Joe Tenczar
leadership.role: Chief Information Officer, CTH and SFG
leadership.background: Previously served as Chief Strategy Officer and CIO for Sonny's Franchise Company.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs technology systems, POS platforms, and information security.

cohorts.label: Model Parks Gross Sales
cohorts.basis: Average Gross Sales of 34 Model Parks open and operating throughout the 2025 fiscal year.

cohorts.label: Model Parks EBITDA
cohorts.basis: Average EBITDA of 34 Model Parks open and operating throughout the 2025 fiscal year.

cohorts.label: Franchised Parks (System-Wide) Gross Sales
cohorts.basis: Average Gross Sales of 106 franchisee-owned parks in the United States during the 2025 fiscal year.

cohorts.label: Franchised Parks (System-Wide) EBITDA
cohorts.basis: Average EBITDA of 106 franchisee-owned parks in the United States during the 2025 fiscal year.

cohorts.label: Corporate Parks Gross Sales
cohorts.basis: Average Gross Sales of 110 corporate-owned parks in the United States during the 2025 fiscal year.

item19.notes: Model Parks are franchisee-owned parks that have at least 25,000 square feet of space, have 4 or more private party rooms, and were open and operating throughout the Reporting Period.

lineItems.notes: Uniform fee paid upon signing the Franchise Agreement.
Estimated at one to two months' rent.
Varies widely by size of space and local construction rates.
Purchased from affiliate Sky Zone, LLC.
Purchased from designated vendors.
Flooring materials as specified in the Operations Manual.
Lighting equipment from designated vendor.
Includes exterior sign and branded graphics package.
Required fire alarms, sprinkler systems, and security systems.
Ovens, freezers, ice makers, and refrigerators from approved vendor.
Technology package including POS, surveillance, and AV equipment.
Paid directly to approved architect.
Estimated building and health permits.
Initial business licenses, food establishment licenses, etc.
Initial supply of food and beverage items.
Wristbands, SkySocks, uniforms, and party supplies.
Party carts, floor scrubber, safety barriers, AED, etc.
Travel, lodging, and pre-opening wages for employees.
Legal and accounting fees to establish business.
Mandatory Master Insurance Program costs.
Working capital for initial three months of operation.
Minimum required spend on grand opening advertising.
Technology fee paid beginning two months prior to opening.
Paid to us no later than 10 days before opening.

hiddenCosts.name: Master Insurance Program Costs
hiddenCosts.description: Total Cost of Risk, currently between 2.4% and 13.0% of Gross Sales, subject to recalculation every six months.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Compliance Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $250 for each day you remain out of compliance; $2,500 per violation for failure to obtain guest waivers.

hiddenCosts.name: Management Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 50% of Gross Sales plus travel and living expenses if the franchisor assumes operation of the park upon default.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by arbitration and/or litigation only in Texas, which may be more costly and less favorable.

operationalRisks.title: Supplier Control
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must purchase nearly all inventory and supplies from the franchisor or designated suppliers at prices they set.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Insurance Program
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must participate in the Master Insurance Program and pay a Total Cost of Risk that can fluctuate significantly.

extracted.conceptRationale: Sky Zone is an indoor trampoline and active entertainment park that includes party rooms and a concession/café area.
extracted.staffingRationale: The business requires a full management team, court monitors, and café staff to supervise shifts and operate the attractions safely.

riskReasons: Capital gap of $4,573,185 is ~18x your liquid — extreme leverage; the deal rests almost entirely on outside capital.
A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $4,823,185 mid-point build-out, there is a $4,573,185 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $173,759/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptLabel: Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)
insights.conceptRationale: Sky Zone is an indoor trampoline and active entertainment park that includes party rooms and a concession/café area.

benchmark.label: Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Blended margin. The bar/kitchen attach rate swings COGS up; the large footprint plus simulator/equipment R&M swing fixed costs. Owner-operator vs. absentee is a full GM salary either way.
benchmark.rampNote: 6–18 months to a mature membership/booking base; the Item 19 average is NOT a year-one number.
considerations: Budget blended COGS ~15–28% (sim time and memberships carry little; the bar and kitchen drive it) and labor ~22–30%.
Decide owner-operator vs. absentee before modeling — absentee adds a GM salary (~$60–90K/yr) the pro forma omits.
Confirm the F&B attach rate and per-bay equipment costs (e.g. simulator licenses, R&M) at mature franchised units via Item 20.
Assume a 6–18 month ramp; discount the Item 19 mature average for year one.

insights.disclosedMarginSource: Model Parks EBITDA ÷ Model Parks Gross Sales

crossCheck.message: The franchisor's disclosed margin (25%) is consistent with the typical 15–26% band for this concept — a positive signal on the reported economics. Still verify against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20).

buildup.label: × franchisor's disclosed operating margin (25%)
buildup.note: Model Parks EBITDA ÷ Model Parks Gross Sales

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Taken straight from the franchisor's Item 19 disclosure (Model Parks EBITDA ÷ Model Parks Gross Sales); no benchmark applied.
Our calculation — the disclosed margin applied to the modeled franchised gross. The margin is from the FDD; this dollar figure is computed, not disclosed.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so CircusTrix Holdings, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
